name: Roast Goose with Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the neck and gizzard and place them in a saucepan with about 1 quart of water. Simmer lightly for several hours, partially covered, until reduced to about 2 cups. Season with salt.
2. Mix all remaining ingredients together except for gravy, adjusting seasoning by tasting.
3. Stuff, lace, and truss the bird. Roast in an oven set to 325°F (breast down) for 1½ hours. As fat accumulates, turn the bird and roast another 1½ hours (or longer if using a larger bird) until juices run clear when pricked where the thigh attaches to the body.
4. Remove from oven and let rest on a heated serving platter while preparing gravy.
5. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of fat, sprinkle with flour, and set the roasting pan over low heat. Stir for one minute, scraping up brown bits.
6. Add broth and stir until smooth. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve in a boat with goose.
Bon Appetit
